page: Microsoft Azure FX24ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 12 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations.

The server is configured with 504.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per physical core. While the instance does not include local storage, it is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance external storage volumes. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the FX24ms_v2 demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, achieving a Geekbench single-core score of 2128.0 and a top-tier text processing score of 2965.0. Multi-core performance is solid, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 15360.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 41565.32. Memory read bandwidth reaches 2073.56 GB/sec for small cached blocks, though uncached large block write bandwidth is lower at 54.55 GB/sec. The system also shows strong performance in LLM inference, processing 2504.27 tokens/sec for small model prompts and 420.68 tokens/sec for text generation.

Given its high memory density of 21.0 GB per core and dedicated CPU allocation, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe drives. The combination of high clock speed, AMX instructions, and massive memory capacity makes it highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, memory-intensive data processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure FX24ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 504.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per physical core. It lacks local storage but is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core execution, highlighted by a top-tier Geekbench text processing score and strong LLM inference capabilities, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU resources without requiring local NVMe disks. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale data processing, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, designed for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and massive RAM capacity for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
504.0 GB of RAM providing 21.0 GB per physical core
Premium Storage capable with no local storage included
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Strong single-core performance and high-speed LLM text generation
Optimized for in-memory databases and memory-intensive enterprise applications
